goal: Intent A: import graph as Structure view-method (Language.import_queries + Structure.imports/dependency_graph)

summary.title: Import graph as Structure view-method (Intent A of packages redesign)
summary.what: Adds Import StrEnum, Language.import_queries() + Language.resolve_module() hooks with per-grammar overrides for all 11 supported languages, ParseResult.root_node persistence, Structure.imports() and Structure.dependency_graph() view methods, Import + DependencyGraph frozen records, and slop.structure._imports helper module. 22 new tests pass (15 per-language import extraction, 7 dependency-graph resolution).
summary.why: structural.packages.rigidity/uselessness rules currently passthrough to slop._structural.robert.robert_kernel — a 1087-line pre-substrate kernel. A line-for-line port would re-import duplicate substrate logic (file discovery, language resolution, import-graph aggregation). The user agreed to redesign these rules as v2.0-native features. Intent A is the foundational primitive — an import-graph view-method — which Intent B will consume alongside new abstractness + package-resolution vocabulary. Splitting A from B ships an independently useful capability (future cycle-detection and layer-violation rules can also consume Structure.dependency_graph) before the full packages-metric rewire.
summary.user_goal: Intent A: import graph as Structure view-method (Language.import_queries + Structure.imports/dependency_graph)

decisions.chose: Tree-sitter query-based import extraction with kind labels preserved on every Import record
decisions.rationale: Mirrors the legacy IMPORT_QUERIES dict's structure (legacy already uses tree-sitter queries) but lifts the per-grammar metadata onto Language.import_queries(). Kind labels (import / from_import / esm / require / include_local / include_system / ruby_require / use / etc.) are kept on each edge because future rules (cycle classification, local-vs-system include policy, require_relative-vs-require Ruby splits) will want to discriminate, and one extra string per edge is negligible cost. User confirmed via AskUserQuestion.

decisions.chose: Dropped the regex text-fallback from the legacy deps_kernel
decisions.rationale: The v2.0 substrate's premise is tree-sitter parsing — files that fail to parse never reach Tree.records. Maintaining a parallel regex-based extraction path would split the source of truth. Manual diff on slop's own source (Python only): new resolver picks up 134 __init__.py re-exports legacy missed, while 24 legacy-only edges are stem-collision false positives (e.g. 'records' resolving incorrectly across tree/records.py and structure/records.py). Net: new is more accurate. User confirmed via AskUserQuestion.

Tree-sitter queries need a parsed tree to run against. Persisting the root node from the existing parse (instead of re-parsing via _ast.query.query_kernel) makes Structure.imports() free-of-extra-IO and unlocks future query-based view methods. Zero memory cost — the tree was already parsed.

Most grammars share the same name→file resolution shape: try the raw module string, then the trailing segment after the rightmost '.' / '::' / '/'. Languages whose import strings need specialised handling (Go: quote-stripped already by the walker; C/C++ paths; Ruby require_relative) can override. Keeping the default thin makes it readable; grammar-specific overrides are localized.

review_notes: Parity divergence vs legacy deps_kernel is documented in the commit message and tilts in our favour (134 new edges captured, 24 legacy false positives dropped). Not a regression.

Pre-existing circular import in slop.cli (slop.linter.format → cli.color → cli._common → slop.linter.format) is unchanged. Full test suite passes (527/527) when run as a whole; only specific test subsets trigger the import-order failure.

Rust import_queries gain a tree-sitter query (the legacy deps_kernel only had a regex text fallback for Rust). The new query covers scoped_identifier, identifier, use_as_clause, and scoped_use_list shapes; minor coverage gap on the rarer wildcard form `use foo::*` is acceptable for Intent B's purposes (the leading crate is what packages-metric needs).

architectural_claims: Import-graph extraction is substrate-aligned: per-grammar tree-sitter queries declared on Language, view-method on Structure, module resolution per-grammar with sane default. No regex fallback.
ParseResult now carries the tree-sitter root node so query-based view methods avoid re-parsing.
Per-grammar kind labels are preserved on Import records to enable future discrimination by import flavour (system vs local include, require vs require_relative, etc.).
behavioral_changes: Structure gains imports() and dependency_graph() methods. No existing rule consumes them yet.
ParseResult gains a root_node field. Existing code that constructs ParseResult continues to work via the default value (None).
migration_notes: No user-facing migration. The legacy slop._structural.deps.deps_kernel remains in place — still consumed by structural.deps cycle rule. Intent B retires robert_kernel but not deps_kernel; the cycles rule retirement is deferred to a separate intent.
